claim: COVID-19 vaccines have been tested in clinical trials
evidence: Multiple phase 3 clinical trials involving tens of thousands of participants confirmed vaccine safety and efficacy before authorization.
label: TRUE

claim: Drinking bleach cures COVID-19
evidence: Medical authorities including WHO and CDC have explicitly warned that ingesting bleach or disinfectants is dangerous and can be fatal.
label: FALSE

claim: Vaccines cause autism
evidence: A large-scale review of over 1.2 million children found no link between MMR vaccination and autism spectrum disorder.
label: FALSE

claim: Climate change is causing rising sea levels
evidence: Satellite data and tide gauge records show global mean sea level has risen approximately 3.6 mm per year since the 1990s.
label: TRUE

claim: The Earth is flat
evidence: Multiple independent observations including satellite imagery, circumnavigation, physics, and astronomy confirm Earth is an oblate spheroid.
label: FALSE

claim: 5G towers spread coronavirus
evidence: Viruses are biological entities that cannot travel through radio waves. No scientific evidence supports any connection between 5G and COVID-19.
label: FALSE

claim: Humans are responsible for accelerating climate change
evidence: The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report states with high confidence that human influence has warmed the climate at an unprecedented rate.
label: TRUE

claim: Election machines were hacked in 2020
evidence: Extensive audits, recounts, and court reviews in multiple states found no evidence of widespread voting machine manipulation in the 2020 US election.
label: FALSE

claim: Voter fraud altered the outcome of the 2020 US election
evidence: Over 60 court cases and multiple state audits found no evidence of fraud sufficient to alter election results.
label: FALSE

claim: The moon landing was faked
evidence: NASA has released thousands of documents, photos, and samples from Apollo missions. Independent verification from observatories worldwide confirmed the landings.
label: FALSE

claim: Drinking water fluoridation is safe at recommended levels
evidence: The CDC and WHO affirm that community water fluoridation at 0.7 mg/L is safe and effective at reducing tooth decay.
label: TRUE

claim: Microchips are in COVID vaccines
evidence: Vaccine ingredients are publicly disclosed and include mRNA, lipid nanoparticles, salts, and sugars. No microchips have been found in any analysis.
label: FALSE

claim: Bitcoin uses significant amounts of electricity
evidence: Cambridge Centre for Alternative Finance estimates Bitcoin's annual electricity consumption rivals that of some medium-sized countries.
label: TRUE

claim: Artificial intelligence will replace all human jobs
evidence: Economists and AI researchers broadly agree AI will automate some tasks but also create new job categories. Complete job replacement is not supported by evidence.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Social media algorithms promote divisive content
evidence: Research from NYU and other institutions found algorithmic amplification correlates with increased engagement on emotionally charged and polarizing content.
label: TRUE

claim: Eating red meat increases cancer risk
evidence: The World Health Organization has classified processed meat as a Group 1 carcinogen and red meat as a Group 2A carcinogen based on epidemiological evidence.
label: TRUE

claim: Bill Gates created COVID-19 to implant microchips
evidence: There is no credible evidence linking Bill Gates to COVID-19 origins. The claim originates from debunked conspiracy sources.
label: FALSE

claim: Immigration increases crime rates
evidence: Multiple peer-reviewed studies including Cato Institute research found that immigrants, including undocumented immigrants, commit crimes at lower rates than native-born citizens.
label: FALSE

claim: Tax cuts always increase economic growth
evidence: Economic research shows mixed results; while some tax cuts stimulate growth, the relationship is complex and depends heavily on implementation and economic conditions.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The global economy is controlled by a secret group
evidence: No credible evidence supports the existence of a unified secret organization controlling global economics. Decisions are made by many competing governments and institutions.
label: FALSE

claim: Renewable energy is becoming cheaper than fossil fuels
evidence: IRENA data shows solar PV and onshore wind are now the cheapest sources of new electricity generation in most of the world.
label: TRUE

claim: Sugar causes hyperactivity in children
evidence: Multiple double-blind studies have found no causal link between sugar consumption and hyperactivity in children.
label: FALSE

claim: George Soros controls world governments
evidence: No credible evidence supports this claim. Soros funds various NGOs and political causes but has no governmental control mechanism.
label: FALSE

claim: Antidepressants are effective for major depression
evidence: Meta-analyses of clinical trial data confirm antidepressants show statistically significant improvements in moderate to severe depression compared to placebo.
label: TRUE

claim: The ozone layer is recovering
evidence: NASA and NOAA data confirm the ozone layer is showing signs of recovery since the Montreal Protocol banned ozone-depleting substances.
label: TRUE

claim: Genetically modified foods are unsafe to eat
evidence: Scientific consensus from WHO, NAS, and over 2000 studies supports the safety of approved GMO foods for human consumption.
label: FALSE

claim: Cryptocurrencies are completely anonymous
evidence: Most major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin use public ledgers. Law enforcement has successfully traced and prosecuted numerous cryptocurrency-related crimes.
label: FALSE

claim: Herd immunity requires 95% vaccination
evidence: Herd immunity thresholds vary by disease; measles requires ~95% but COVID-19 herd immunity estimates range widely depending on variant and immunity type.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: China has the world's largest economy by GDP PPP
evidence: IMF data shows China surpassed the United States in GDP measured by purchasing power parity around 2016.
label: TRUE

claim: The US dollar will collapse in five years
evidence: No credible economic institution has forecasted a US dollar collapse. While the dollar faces long-term reserve currency challenges, collapse predictions lack supporting evidence.
label: FALSE

claim: Deep-sea mining could damage ocean ecosystems
evidence: Scientific studies indicate deep-sea mining operations disturb unique ecosystems and can have far-reaching ecological impacts.
label: TRUE

claim: Social media causes depression in teenagers
evidence: Multiple longitudinal studies have found associations between heavy social media use and increased depression symptoms in adolescents, particularly among girls.
label: TRUE

claim: Schools are indoctrinating children with political ideology
evidence: Broad claims of systematic political indoctrination in schools lack specific evidence. Individual incidents exist but do not represent systemic national policy.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Nuclear power produces zero carbon emissions
evidence: While nuclear plants produce negligible direct emissions, lifecycle analysis including construction and fuel processing yields low but non-zero carbon footprints.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Electric vehicles are worse for the environment than gas cars
evidence: Lifecycle analyses show EVs have lower total carbon emissions than comparable gas vehicles in most regions, though manufacturing impacts are higher.
label: FALSE

claim: Inflation is caused by money printing
evidence: While excessive money supply growth can cause inflation, inflation is a complex phenomenon influenced by supply chains, demand, energy prices, and other factors.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The pharmaceutical industry suppresses cancer cures
evidence: No credible evidence supports systematic suppression of cancer cures. Multiple effective cancer treatments have been developed and commercialized over decades.
label: FALSE

claim: AI can outperform doctors at diagnosing certain cancers
evidence: Peer-reviewed studies have shown AI systems matching or exceeding radiologist performance at detecting specific cancers in imaging studies.
label: TRUE

claim: The internet was invented by Al Gore
evidence: Al Gore supported legislation that funded early internet infrastructure, but the internet was invented by DARPA researchers including Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn.
label: FALSE

claim: Facebook sells your personal data directly to advertisers
evidence: Facebook does not sell user data directly; it uses that data internally to target ads on behalf of advertisers.
label: FALSE

claim: Wearing masks reduces COVID-19 transmission
evidence: Multiple studies and meta-analyses found that mask use, particularly N95/FFP2 respirators, reduces transmission of respiratory viruses including SARS-CoV-2.
label: TRUE

claim: Homeopathy is scientifically effective
evidence: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses consistently find homeopathic treatments perform no better than placebo.
label: FALSE

claim: China is the world's largest carbon emitter
evidence: IEA and Global Carbon Project data confirm China emits the most CO2 annually of any single country.
label: TRUE

claim: Marijuana is a gateway drug
evidence: Research shows correlation between marijuana use and use of other substances, but causal gateway effect evidence is weak and disputed among researchers.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The US healthcare system has the best outcomes globally
evidence: WHO and OECD data show the US ranks poorly on many health outcome metrics despite highest per-capita spending.
label: FALSE

claim: Hackers can access any computer remotely
evidence: While sophisticated cyber attacks are real, accessing any arbitrary computer remotely requires exploiting specific vulnerabilities, not universal capability.
label: FALSE

claim: Ransomware attacks on hospitals have increased
evidence: Cybersecurity firms including Mandiant and Recorded Future documented significant increases in ransomware attacks targeting healthcare infrastructure.
label: TRUE

claim: Phishing is the most common cyberattack vector
evidence: Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report consistently lists phishing as the top initial access vector across industries.
label: TRUE

claim: Zero-day vulnerabilities are sold on the dark web
evidence: Law enforcement agencies and cybersecurity researchers have documented markets for zero-day exploits operating on both dark web and gray market platforms.
label: TRUE

claim: Antivirus software provides complete protection
evidence: Security researchers universally state that no single tool provides complete protection; layered security approaches are necessary.
label: FALSE

claim: Two-factor authentication prevents all account breaches
evidence: While 2FA significantly reduces account compromise risk, sophisticated attacks including SIM swapping and man-in-the-middle attacks can bypass some 2FA methods.
label: FALSE

claim: Open source software is more secure than proprietary software
evidence: Security research shows neither model is inherently more secure; both have significant vulnerability histories. Open source benefits from community review.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: China-linked hackers conduct state-sponsored espionage
evidence: US intelligence agencies and cybersecurity firms including Mandiant have extensively documented APT groups linked to Chinese state interests.
label: TRUE

claim: Quantum computing will break all encryption
evidence: Quantum computers could theoretically break certain encryption schemes, but current quantum computers lack sufficient stable qubits for this. Post-quantum cryptography is being developed.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Data breaches expose billions of records annually
evidence: ITRC and IBM Cost of a Data Breach reports document billions of records exposed annually across thousands of incidents.
label: TRUE

claim: VPNs make you completely anonymous online
evidence: VPNs encrypt traffic and mask IP addresses but do not provide complete anonymity; VPN providers may log activity and users can still be identified through other means.
label: FALSE

claim: IoT devices are frequently compromised in botnets
evidence: Mirai botnet and subsequent campaigns demonstrated mass compromise of IoT devices. CISA regularly issues advisories on IoT security vulnerabilities.
label: TRUE

claim: Inflation hit a 40-year high in the US in 2022
evidence: US Bureau of Labor Statistics data confirmed CPI inflation reached 9.1% in June 2022, the highest rate since 1981.
label: TRUE

claim: The stock market always recovers from crashes
evidence: Historical S&P 500 data shows the index has recovered from every major crash over long time horizons, though recovery timelines vary significantly.
label: TRUE

claim: Cryptocurrency is primarily used for crime
evidence: Chainalysis blockchain analytics data shows illicit activity represents a small percentage of total crypto transaction volume.
label: FALSE

claim: Interest rate hikes always cause recessions
evidence: While rate hikes can slow economic growth, the relationship is not deterministic. Some tightening cycles have resulted in soft landings.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The Federal Reserve is a private entity
evidence: The Federal Reserve System has both public and private elements; regional Fed banks are owned by member banks but the Board of Governors is a federal agency.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Investing in index funds outperforms most active funds
evidence: Multiple studies including Morningstar's SPIVA scorecard consistently show majority of active funds underperform their benchmark index over long periods.
label: TRUE

claim: Gold is a reliable hedge against inflation
evidence: Historical data shows gold's correlation with inflation is inconsistent; it has performed well in some inflationary periods but not others.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Student loan debt exceeds one trillion dollars in the US
evidence: Federal Reserve data confirms US student loan debt surpassed $1.7 trillion.
label: TRUE

claim: The housing market will crash 50% soon
evidence: No credible economic institution has issued such a forecast. Housing market predictions are inherently uncertain and depend on many variables.
label: FALSE

claim: Payday loans trap borrowers in debt cycles
evidence: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau research found a majority of payday loan volume comes from borrowers who rollover loans repeatedly.
label: TRUE

claim: Universal basic income eliminates the incentive to work
evidence: Pilot programs in Finland, Kenya, and Stockton California showed mixed results on work incentives, with many participants continuing to work.
label: FALSE

claim: China is buying all US farmland
evidence: Foreign ownership of US agricultural land including Chinese entities represents a small fraction of total farmland. USDA data confirms this.
label: FALSE

claim: Voter ID laws disproportionately affect minorities
evidence: Research from the Government Accountability Office and academic institutions found voter ID requirements have differential impacts on minority voter participation.
label: TRUE

claim: Mail-in voting is susceptible to widespread fraud
evidence: Studies of mail voting in states with long histories of all-mail elections found fraud rates are extremely low, typically less than 0.0025%.
label: FALSE

claim: Gerrymandering distorts electoral outcomes
evidence: Academic research including work by political scientists at Princeton confirms partisan gerrymandering significantly affects electoral map fairness.
label: TRUE

claim: Citizens United allows unlimited dark money in politics
evidence: The Supreme Court Citizens United decision opened pathways for unlimited independent expenditures, though direct candidate contributions remain limited.
label: TRUE

claim: Foreign governments interfere in US elections
evidence: US intelligence community assessments have documented Russian, Iranian, and Chinese interference activities targeting US elections.
label: TRUE

claim: Early voting increases voter turnout
evidence: Research on early voting effects on turnout shows mixed results; some studies find modest positive effects while others find minimal impact.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Electronic voting machines have been hacked
evidence: DEFCON Voting Village has demonstrated vulnerabilities in voting machine software. However, documented cases of actual election outcome alteration via hacking are rare.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Social media platforms censor conservative voices
evidence: Studies including from NYU Center for Business and Human Rights found conservative content among the most engaged on Facebook, challenging systematic censorship claims.
label: FALSE

claim: Online misinformation spreads faster than truth
evidence: MIT Media Lab study in Science journal found false news spreads faster and more broadly than true news on social media platforms.
label: TRUE

claim: Facebook knew about teen mental health harms
evidence: Leaked Facebook internal research documents published by WSJ showed the company had conducted research on Instagram's negative effects on teenage girls.
label: TRUE

claim: Twitter bots amplify political disinformation
evidence: Research from Oxford Internet Institute and Stanford Internet Observatory documented inauthentic bot networks amplifying political content on Twitter.
label: TRUE

claim: TikTok collects excessive user data
evidence: FTC and multiple cybersecurity analyses have found TikTok collects extensive device and usage data, and ByteDance's relationship with Chinese authorities raises concerns.
label: TRUE

claim: YouTube's algorithm radicalizes viewers
evidence: Researchers including Amin Hosseini and others have found evidence of YouTube's recommendation algorithm leading viewers toward increasingly extreme content.
label: TRUE

claim: Instagram filters harm body image
evidence: Psychological research published in peer-reviewed journals has found exposure to filtered and idealized images on social media correlates with body dissatisfaction.
label: TRUE

claim: Deepfake technology makes video evidence unreliable
evidence: AI-generated synthetic media has advanced to where visual artifacts are difficult to detect, prompting development of forensic detection tools.
label: TRUE

claim: Elon Musk bought Twitter to influence elections
evidence: Musk has stated his motivations relate to free speech. His actual motivations are subject to interpretation; direct evidence of election influence intent is limited.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Social media platforms are regulated like publishers
evidence: In the US, Section 230 gives platforms liability protections beyond traditional publisher status. The legal classification is actively debated.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Online harassment is predominantly directed at women
evidence: Pew Research Center studies consistently find women experience certain forms of online harassment including sexual harassment at higher rates than men.
label: TRUE

claim: Climate scientists face death threats for their work
evidence: Documented cases show climate researchers have received threats and harassment campaigns. Major scientific bodies have addressed researcher safety concerns.
label: TRUE

claim: Arctic sea ice is declining
evidence: NASA and NSIDC satellite data confirm long-term decline in Arctic sea ice extent and thickness.
label: TRUE

claim: Plastic pollution threatens marine ecosystems
evidence: Scientific studies document widespread microplastic contamination in marine environments and documented impacts on marine organisms.
label: TRUE

claim: Wind turbines cause bird deaths
evidence: Wind turbines do kill birds, but studies show cats, buildings, and vehicles kill far more birds than turbines annually.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Scientists are paid to support climate change
evidence: Climate researchers receive grants based on research quality, not conclusions. No credible evidence of coordinated payment for specific conclusions exists.
label: FALSE

claim: Deforestation contributes to climate change
evidence: Scientific consensus confirms forest loss reduces carbon sequestration capacity and increases atmospheric CO2 concentration.
label: TRUE

claim: Ocean acidification threatens coral reefs
evidence: NOAA and peer-reviewed research documents increasing ocean acidity and its destructive effects on coral calcification.
label: TRUE

claim: Biodiversity is declining at unprecedented rates
evidence: UN IPBES Global Assessment found species extinction rates are accelerating, with approximately one million species threatened.
label: TRUE

claim: Lab-grown meat is environmentally superior to beef
evidence: Life cycle analyses show cultivated meat has significantly lower land use but energy consumption comparisons are complex and depend on electricity source.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Intermittent fasting improves metabolic health
evidence: Multiple randomized controlled trials show intermittent fasting produces comparable weight loss and metabolic improvements to continuous calorie restriction.
label: TRUE

claim: Coffee reduces risk of type 2 diabetes
evidence: Multiple prospective cohort studies found inverse associations between coffee consumption and type 2 diabetes risk.
label: TRUE

claim: Sitting is the new smoking
evidence: While prolonged sedentary behavior is associated with health risks, comparisons to smoking in terms of mortality risk are not supported by direct epidemiological equivalence.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Meditation reduces anxiety
evidence: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses of randomized trials show mindfulness meditation produces significant reductions in anxiety symptoms.
label: TRUE

claim: Sunscreen causes vitamin D deficiency
evidence: While sunscreen reduces vitamin D synthesis, typical usage does not completely block synthesis and most people can obtain vitamin D through diet and limited sun exposure.
label: FALSE

claim: Antibiotics are ineffective against viruses
evidence: Antibiotics target bacterial cell structures and processes and have no mechanism of action against viral infections.
label: TRUE

claim: Antibiotic resistance is a global health crisis
evidence: WHO and CDC classify antimicrobial resistance as one of the most urgent global health threats, with drug-resistant infections causing hundreds of thousands of deaths annually.
label: TRUE

claim: Sleep deprivation impairs cognitive function
evidence: Extensive laboratory research demonstrates sleep restriction significantly impairs attention, working memory, reaction time, and decision-making.
label: TRUE

claim: Exercise reduces risk of cardiovascular disease
evidence: Decades of epidemiological research and clinical trials confirm regular physical activity significantly reduces cardiovascular disease risk.
label: TRUE

claim: Ultra-processed foods are associated with poor health outcomes
evidence: Multiple large cohort studies associate high ultra-processed food consumption with increased risk of obesity, cardiovascular disease, and mortality.
label: TRUE

claim: E-cigarettes are safer than traditional cigarettes
evidence: While e-cigarettes appear to expose users to fewer harmful chemicals than combustible cigarettes, they are not without health risks, particularly for youth.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Autism rates are increasing
evidence: Reported autism diagnosis rates have increased significantly, though researchers attribute much of this to expanded diagnostic criteria and increased awareness.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Reading improves cognitive function
evidence: Longitudinal research finds regular reading associated with larger vocabulary, improved comprehension, and cognitive preservation in aging.
label: TRUE

claim: College education guarantees higher earnings
evidence: Statistical data shows college graduates earn more on average, but outcomes vary significantly by field, institution, and individual circumstances.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Student performance is declining in developed nations
evidence: PISA international assessment data shows mixed trends; some countries show declines while others show improvements in student performance.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Private schools outperform public schools
evidence: Research controlling for socioeconomic factors shows smaller or no performance gaps between private and public schools than raw score comparisons suggest.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Early childhood education has lifelong benefits
evidence: Longitudinal studies including the HighScope Perry Preschool Study found early quality education produced positive lifetime outcomes in education, employment, and reduced crime.
label: TRUE

claim: Teachers are underpaid in the United States
evidence: Bureau of Labor Statistics data and economic research suggests teacher compensation relative to comparable professions has declined over several decades.
label: TRUE

claim: Online learning is as effective as in-person education
evidence: Research shows online learning effectiveness varies significantly by subject, student characteristics, and program quality. Direct equivalence is not established.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Critical race theory is being taught in K-12 schools
evidence: CRT is a graduate-level legal framework. Most K-12 diversity curricula involve age-appropriate history rather than formal CRT.
label: FALSE

claim: School voucher programs improve educational outcomes
evidence: Research on voucher programs shows mixed results; some studies find modest gains while others find neutral or negative effects on academic achievement.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Libraries are facing budget cuts across the US
evidence: ALA annual surveys document widespread library budget reductions affecting staffing, hours, and collections.
label: TRUE

claim: Gene editing with CRISPR could cure genetic diseases
evidence: Multiple clinical trials using CRISPR-based therapies have shown promising results for sickle cell disease and other genetic conditions.
label: TRUE

claim: Space exploration benefits everyday life
evidence: Technologies developed for space programs have produced numerous practical applications including GPS, satellite weather, and many medical devices.
label: TRUE

claim: Nuclear fusion is nearly commercially viable
evidence: While fusion research has made significant progress including NIF ignition milestone, commercial fusion power remains at least a decade or more away.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Microplastics have been found in human blood
evidence: A 2022 study published in Environment International confirmed detection of microplastic particles in human blood samples.
label: TRUE

claim: PFAS chemicals are widespread environmental contaminants
evidence: EPA and academic research confirm PFAS contamination in water supplies, soil, and human blood across the United States and globally.
label: TRUE

claim: Antibiotic-resistant superbugs are emerging
evidence: CDC and WHO document emergence of pathogens resistant to multiple antibiotics, with some strains showing resistance to all available treatments.
label: TRUE

claim: Telehealth improves healthcare access
evidence: Studies on telehealth adoption during and after COVID-19 found increased healthcare utilization particularly in rural and underserved areas.
label: TRUE

claim: US healthcare costs are the highest globally
evidence: OECD data consistently shows the US spends more per capita on healthcare than any other developed nation.
label: TRUE

claim: Mental health conditions are undertreated worldwide
evidence: WHO estimates more than 75% of people with mental disorders in low and middle income countries receive no treatment.
label: TRUE

claim: Loneliness is a public health crisis
evidence: US Surgeon General issued an advisory declaring loneliness and isolation epidemic and citing significant health impacts comparable to smoking.
label: TRUE

claim: AI bias perpetuates racial discrimination
evidence: Research including ProPublica's COMPAS analysis and MIT Media Lab's Gender Shades project documented racial and gender bias in AI systems.
label: TRUE

claim: Autonomous vehicles will eliminate traffic deaths
evidence: While AVs could reduce some human error-related accidents, achieving zero traffic deaths via autonomous vehicles faces substantial technical and regulatory challenges.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Facial recognition technology misidentifies minorities
evidence: NIST testing found significant performance disparities across demographic groups in facial recognition algorithms, with higher error rates for darker-skinned individuals.
label: TRUE

claim: Blockchain is the solution to all data integrity problems
evidence: While blockchain offers certain tamper-resistance properties, it is not universally superior to traditional databases and has significant scaling and efficiency limitations.
label: FALSE

claim: Tech monopolies stifle innovation
evidence: Economic research on platform market dynamics shows mixed evidence; some studies find incumbents acquire and suppress rivals while others find continued market dynamism.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Social media companies profit from teen addiction
evidence: Internal documents and whistleblower testimony from Facebook indicate awareness of addictive design patterns in Instagram targeting younger users.
label: TRUE

claim: Cybersecurity professionals are in high demand
evidence: US Bureau of Labor Statistics projects information security analyst jobs to grow much faster than average through 2031.
label: TRUE

claim: Most data breaches involve human error
evidence: IBM and Verizon annual reports consistently find human factors involved in a majority of data breaches.
label: TRUE

claim: End-to-end encryption protects privacy
evidence: Properly implemented end-to-end encryption prevents third parties including service providers from reading message content.
label: TRUE

claim: The dark web is primarily used for crime
evidence: While illicit markets operate on dark web networks, research shows substantial legitimate use for privacy, journalism, and circumventing censorship.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Global internet censorship is increasing
evidence: Freedom House's Freedom on the Net report documents increasing internet restrictions in authoritarian and some democratic countries.
label: TRUE

claim: China has the most internet users
evidence: ITU data confirms China has the world's largest online population.
label: TRUE

claim: Remote work increases productivity
evidence: Research on remote work shows mixed results; some studies show productivity gains while others show losses depending on role type, home environment, and management.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Income inequality is rising globally
evidence: World Inequality Lab and Oxfam reports using tax data and surveys document increasing income and wealth concentration in most economies.
label: TRUE

claim: Minimum wage increases cause unemployment
evidence: Economic research on minimum wage effects is contested; studies show some negative employment effects in certain sectors while others find minimal impact.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Universal healthcare would reduce US costs
evidence: Comparative international data and economic modeling studies show mixed conclusions on whether single-payer would reduce overall US healthcare costs.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Free trade increases global prosperity
evidence: Mainstream economics supports free trade benefits for aggregate GDP, though distributional effects on workers in affected industries can be significantly negative.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: US national debt threatens economic stability
evidence: Economists debate debt threshold risks; while large debt levels create risks, the US dollar's reserve currency status provides significant mitigation.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Inflation reduces purchasing power
evidence: By definition inflation represents the rate at which currency purchasing power declines over time.
label: TRUE

claim: ESG investing sacrifices returns
evidence: Research on ESG fund performance shows mixed results compared to conventional funds, with methodological challenges in attribution.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Real estate is always a good investment
evidence: While real estate has historically appreciated, outcomes vary significantly by location, timing, leverage, and management. Guaranteed returns are not supported by evidence.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Private equity firms improve company performance
evidence: Academic research on private equity portfolio company performance shows mixed results on productivity and employment, with strong returns to investors but some concerns about debt loading.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Immigration grows the economy
evidence: Mainstream economic research finds immigration generally has positive macroeconomic effects through labor supply, entrepreneurship, and innovation.
label: TRUE

claim: Automation is causing wage stagnation
evidence: Economic research finds some association between automation and wage pressure in routine task occupations, contributing to widening wage inequality.
label: TRUE

claim: The global middle class is shrinking
evidence: World Bank data actually shows significant growth of the global middle class over past decades, though gains are unevenly distributed.
label: FALSE

claim: Circular economy practices reduce waste
evidence: Life cycle analyses of circular economy business models show significant material and energy efficiency gains versus linear production models.
label: TRUE

claim: Vertical farming can feed cities
evidence: Research on controlled environment agriculture shows high yields per square foot but energy costs and protein crop limitations remain significant challenges.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Rewilding restores ecosystems
evidence: Ecological studies of rewilding projects in Europe and elsewhere show significant biodiversity recovery in targeted areas.
label: TRUE

claim: Carbon capture technology can solve climate change
evidence: Current carbon capture technology exists but operates at insufficient scale and with high energy costs to constitute a complete climate solution.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The Great Barrier Reef is dying
evidence: Australian Institute of Marine Science monitoring shows severe bleaching events driven by warming temperatures have significantly damaged portions of the Great Barrier Reef.
label: TRUE

claim: Bee populations are declining
evidence: USDA and independent monitoring programs document significant honey bee colony losses and wild bee species population declines.
label: TRUE

claim: Soil depletion threatens food security
evidence: FAO reports that agricultural soil degradation is advancing faster than natural regeneration in many regions, threatening long-term crop yields.
label: TRUE

claim: Desalination can solve water scarcity
evidence: While desalination technology is expanding, high energy costs, brine disposal, and geographic limitations prevent it from being a universal solution.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Weather modification technology exists
evidence: Cloud seeding technology exists and is used in various countries. Claims of large-scale weather control beyond precipitation enhancement are not scientifically supported.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Tidal energy is underutilized
evidence: Tidal and wave energy technologies exist with significant theoretical potential but face cost and technology maturity barriers relative to solar and wind.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The US government has evidence of UFO/UAP
evidence: US Congressional testimony and DNI reports confirm the government has documented Unidentified Aerial Phenomena incidents without confirmed explanation.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Area 51 contains alien technology
evidence: No credible evidence of extraterrestrial technology at Area 51. The facility is known to have been used for classified aircraft testing.
label: FALSE

claim: Chemtrails are government chemical spraying
evidence: Atmospheric scientists have explained contrails as water vapor condensation. No credible evidence of deliberate chemical spraying programs.
label: FALSE

claim: HAARP controls weather
evidence: HAARP is a legitimate ionospheric research program. No credible scientific evidence supports weather control capability.
label: FALSE

claim: Secret societies control governments
evidence: While influential networks and organizations exist, no credible evidence supports a unified secret society with governmental control.
label: FALSE

claim: The UN is planning a global government
evidence: No credible documentary evidence supports plans for UN replacement of national governments. Sovereignty remains the foundation of international law.
label: FALSE

claim: NATO expansion provoked Russian aggression
evidence: This is a contested geopolitical interpretation. Scholars debate whether NATO expansion was a significant factor in Russian decision-making versus domestic and imperial motivations.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: China will surpass the US economically
evidence: IMF and World Bank projections show China's GDP on trajectories to potentially surpass US nominal GDP within decades, though outcomes are uncertain.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Russia hacked the 2016 US election
evidence: US Senate Intelligence Committee and Mueller investigation documented Russian interference including hacking and social media operations, though impact on outcome is disputed.
label: TRUE

claim: Cambridge Analytica influenced the Brexit vote
evidence: ICO and parliamentary investigations documented Facebook data misuse by Cambridge Analytica, though direct causal impact on the Brexit outcome is disputed.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Social media caused the Arab Spring
evidence: Social media platforms facilitated coordination and information spread, but underlying political and economic conditions were the primary drivers.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Propaganda is more effective than ever
evidence: Research on computational propaganda shows increasing sophistication and scale of influence operations, though measuring effectiveness is methodologically challenging.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Democracy is in global decline
evidence: Freedom House and V-Dem datasets document a decline in democratic governance indicators across multiple countries over the past decade.
label: TRUE

claim: Authoritarianism is rising globally
evidence: Multiple political science tracking projects document increases in autocratic practices including press restrictions and judicial independence erosion.
label: TRUE

claim: Nuclear proliferation risk is increasing
evidence: Arms control experts cite erosion of nonproliferation frameworks and ongoing programs in North Korea and Iran as increasing proliferation risks.
label: TRUE

claim: Cybersecurity is now a domain of warfare
evidence: NATO, US, and other militaries formally recognize cyberspace as a domain of warfare, alongside land, sea, air, and space.
label: TRUE

claim: State-sponsored disinformation campaigns are common
evidence: Intelligence agencies and academic researchers have documented disinformation campaigns from Russia, China, Iran, and other state actors.
label: TRUE

claim: The WHO is controlled by China
evidence: China is one of many WHO member states. While Chinese influence is a concern raised by critics, WHO decisions involve 194 member states.
label: FALSE

claim: Ivermectin cures COVID-19
evidence: Multiple large randomized controlled trials including the TOGETHER trial found ivermectin did not show significant benefit for COVID-19 treatment.
label: FALSE

claim: Natural immunity is superior to vaccine immunity
evidence: Evidence is mixed; natural infection produces strong immunity but with risks of severe disease. Vaccine immunity is safer. Hybrid immunity shows advantages.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Myocarditis risk from vaccines is higher than from COVID
evidence: Data shows myocarditis risk from COVID-19 infection exceeds vaccine-associated myocarditis risk, particularly in older age groups.
label: FALSE

claim: Vitamin D prevents COVID-19
evidence: While vitamin D deficiency is associated with worse COVID outcomes, clinical trials have not confirmed vitamin D supplementation as a COVID-19 prevention measure.
label: FALSE

claim: mRNA vaccines can alter DNA
evidence: mRNA does not enter the cell nucleus where DNA is stored and cannot reverse transcribe into human DNA under normal cellular conditions.
label: FALSE

claim: Long COVID affects millions
evidence: CDC and NIH estimate millions of Americans experience post-acute COVID symptoms including fatigue, brain fog, and cardiovascular effects.
label: TRUE

claim: Telecom companies lobbied against 5G safety research
evidence: Investigative reporting has documented lobbying efforts by telecom companies on 5G regulation, though direct suppression of safety research is not well documented.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: TikTok is banned in the United States
evidence: Legislation signed in 2024 required ByteDance to divest TikTok or face US ban. Implementation and legal challenges are ongoing.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Elon Musk is the richest person in the world
evidence: Forbes and Bloomberg Billionaires Index have at various times ranked Musk as the world's wealthiest individual; rankings fluctuate with asset values.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Twitter lost advertisers after Musk takeover
evidence: Multiple major brands suspended or reduced Twitter advertising following Musk's acquisition in 2022, citing content moderation concerns.
label: TRUE

claim: Meta is building the metaverse
evidence: Meta has invested heavily in virtual and augmented reality platforms under the metaverse vision, though commercial adoption has been slower than projected.
label: TRUE

claim: Apple has trillion dollar market cap
evidence: Apple has periodically held a market capitalization exceeding one trillion dollars, making it among the first companies to reach that threshold.
label: TRUE

claim: OpenAI developed ChatGPT
evidence: ChatGPT was developed by OpenAI and released publicly in November 2022, becoming the fastest-growing consumer application in history.
label: TRUE

claim: Generative AI creates copyright issues
evidence: Legal cases and academic analysis have raised significant unresolved questions about copyright status of AI training data and AI-generated content.
label: TRUE

claim: AI hallucinations are a fundamental problem
evidence: AI language models produce confident false statements, known as hallucinations, which is a documented and active research area.
label: TRUE

claim: Big tech companies are too powerful
evidence: Antitrust investigations by DOJ, FTC, and EU competition authorities into Google, Apple, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft reflect regulatory concern about market concentration.
label: TRUE

claim: Net neutrality was repealed in the US
evidence: FCC voted to repeal net neutrality rules in 2017. Subsequent legal and regulatory battles have continued over internet service classification.
label: TRUE

claim: 5G networks are faster than 4G
evidence: 5G technology specifications and performance testing confirm significantly higher theoretical and practical speeds compared to 4G LTE networks.
label: TRUE

claim: Robo-advisors outperform human financial advisors
evidence: Research on automated investment services shows comparable risk-adjusted returns to human advisors at lower cost, with significant variation by market conditions.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: High frequency trading destabilizes markets
evidence: SEC analysis and academic research show HFT can reduce spreads in normal conditions but may amplify volatility during market stress events.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The gig economy lacks worker protections
evidence: Research consistently finds gig workers lack standard employment benefits including healthcare, retirement plans, and unemployment insurance in most jurisdictions.
label: TRUE

claim: GDPR has improved data privacy
evidence: European Data Protection Board enforcement data shows increased awareness and some improved corporate data practices, though compliance is uneven.
label: TRUE

claim: Digital divide affects educational outcomes
evidence: Research correlates lack of internet access and devices with lower educational performance, a gap that became particularly visible during pandemic school closures.
label: TRUE

claim: AI tutoring can improve learning outcomes
evidence: Randomized controlled trials of intelligent tutoring systems have shown significant learning gains, particularly in mathematics.
label: TRUE

claim: School lunch nutrition affects academic performance
evidence: Studies find nutritional quality of school meals correlates with student attention, attendance, and test performance.
label: TRUE

claim: Charter schools outperform public schools
evidence: Meta-analyses of charter school performance show high variance; some perform significantly better than comparable public schools while others perform worse.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Teacher diversity improves student outcomes
evidence: Research finds students of color show academic benefits when taught by teachers of similar racial and ethnic backgrounds.
label: TRUE

claim: Arts education improves STEM performance
evidence: Research shows arts education is associated with improved creative thinking and some academic outcomes, though causal mechanisms are debated.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Standardized testing has racial bias
evidence: Psychometric research has documented differential item functioning and score gaps by race on standardized tests, raising validity questions.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: School funding inequality persists in the US
evidence: EdBuild and other nonprofit research organizations document significant per-pupil spending disparities between wealthy and poor school districts.
label: TRUE

claim: Childhood poverty affects brain development
evidence: Neuroscience research using MRI imaging has found measurable differences in brain development associated with childhood poverty and chronic stress.
label: TRUE

claim: Play-based learning is effective
evidence: Early childhood education research supports play-based learning as developmentally appropriate and associated with positive academic and social outcomes.
label: TRUE

claim: College admissions favor legacy applicants
evidence: Research and litigation have documented significant preferences for children of alumni at elite universities, raising equity concerns.
label: TRUE

claim: STEM graduates earn more than humanities graduates
evidence: BLS Occupational Employment Statistics consistently show higher median wages for STEM occupations than many humanities career paths.
label: TRUE

claim: Financial literacy education reduces debt
evidence: Studies on financial education effectiveness show modest positive effects on financial knowledge but more mixed evidence on actual financial behavior changes.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Military spending crowds out social investment
evidence: Economic analysis of opportunity costs shows military expenditure competes with alternative budget priorities, though causal displacement is complex.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: The US military budget exceeds the next ten countries combined
evidence: SIPRI data has shown US defense spending exceeds the combined spending of the next several largest military spenders, though exact comparison depends on methodology.
label: TRUE

claim: NATO allies are not meeting spending commitments
evidence: NATO has documented that many member states do not meet the 2% of GDP defense spending guideline established by the alliance.
label: TRUE

claim: UN peacekeeping missions reduce conflict
evidence: Academic research shows UN peacekeeping operations correlate with reduced conflict recurrence, particularly civil wars, with some heterogeneity by mission type.
label: TRUE

claim: Sanctions are effective at changing state behavior
evidence: Political science research shows economic sanctions have limited success at compelling major policy changes but can impose significant economic costs.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Climate agreements reduce emissions
evidence: Analysis of emissions trends following Paris Agreement implementation shows insufficient progress toward stated targets in most signatory countries.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: International aid reduces poverty
evidence: Development economics research shows targeted aid programs have achieved significant poverty reduction in specific contexts, though general budget aid effectiveness is debated.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Human trafficking is increasing
evidence: UNODC Global Trafficking in Persons Report documents increasing detection of trafficking cases, though this may reflect improved detection rather than purely increased incidence.
label: UNCERTAIN

claim: Drug legalization reduces harm
evidence: Evidence from Portugal's decriminalization and other jurisdictions shows drug policy reform can reduce overdose deaths and incarceration without significant increase in use.
label: TRUE

claim: The opioid epidemic was caused by pharmaceutical marketing
evidence: Congressional investigations and litigation documents confirmed pharmaceutical companies including Purdue Pharma engaged in aggressive marketing downplaying opioid addiction risks.
label: TRUE

claim: Gun violence in the US is uniquely high
evidence: Comparative international data shows US gun violence rates significantly exceed other high-income countries.
label: TRUE

claim: Mental illness does not cause most gun violence
evidence: Research by American Psychological Association and epidemiologists finds most gun violence is not perpetrated by those with diagnosable mental illness.
label: TRUE

claim: Poverty drives crime
evidence: Criminological research finds strong correlations between poverty, inequality, and crime rates, though the causal relationship is complex.
label: TRUE

claim: Mass incarceration disproportionately affects minorities
evidence: Bureau of Justice Statistics data confirms Black and Hispanic Americans are incarcerated at significantly higher rates than white Americans.
label: TRUE

claim: Rehabilitation reduces recidivism
evidence: Meta-analyses of correctional programming show cognitive behavioral therapy and education programs significantly reduce reoffending rates.
label: TRUE

claim: Police funding affects crime rates
evidence: Criminological research on police resource allocation shows mixed findings; some studies find increased staffing reduces crime while others find limited effects.
label: UNCERTAIN
